headline: Bill Ackman Has a Stroke of Genius. Buy His New Fund and Get Shares of His Pershing Square IPO.
summary: Bill Ackman is offering an innovative inducement to get investors to buy a new U.S. closed-end equity fund.  Buyers of the fund will get 20 shares in Ackman’s management company as a bonus for every 100 shares of the closed-end fund that they purchase.  Barron’s estimates that a buyer of 100 shares of the new closed-end fund, Pershing Square USA, for $5,000 ($50 a share) could get $500 in shares of the Ackman’s management company, Pershing Square Inc., or roughly 10% of the purchase price.

raw.headline: Bill Ackman Has a Stroke of Genius. Buy His New Fund and Get Shares of His Pershing Square IPO.

Buyers of the fund will get 20 shares in Ackman’s management company as a bonus for every 100 shares of the closed-end fund that they purchase.  Barron’s estimates that a buyer of 100 shares of the new closed-end fund, Pershing Square USA, for $5,000 ($50 a share) could get $500 in shares of the Ackman’s management company, Pershing Square Inc., or roughly 10% of the purchase price.

headline: Meta to charge advertisers a fee to offset Europe's digital taxes
summary: Meta Platforms will charge advertisers a location fee ranging ‌from 2% to 5% to cover digital ‌service taxes imposed by some countries, the U.S. tech giant said ​in a post on its website, following in the footsteps of Alphabet's  Google and Amazon.  The fee, for image or video ads delivered on Meta platforms including WhatsApp ‌click-to-message campaigns and ⁠marketing messages together with ads, will apply from July 1 and will also ⁠cover other government-imposed levies.  Meta listed six countries where the fees will apply, ‌ranging from 2% in the ​United Kingdom to 3% in ​France, Italy and ​Spain and 5% in Austria and Turkey.

The fee, for image or video ads delivered on Meta platforms including WhatsApp ‌click-to-message campaigns and ⁠marketing messages together with ads, will apply from July 1 and will also ⁠cover other government-imposed levies.  Meta listed six countries where the fees will apply, ‌ranging from 2% in the ​United Kingdom to 3% in ​France, Italy and ​Spain and 5% in Austria and Turkey.

headline: Sony fighting $2.7 billion UK lawsuit over PlayStation Store prices
summary: Sony is fighting a London lawsuit worth almost 2 billion pounds ($2.7 billion) that alleges the PlayStation maker's "monopoly position" inflated prices ‌for digital games, in the latest mass consumer case to go to trial ‌in Britain.  The Japanese conglomerate is accused of abusing its dominant position by requiring digital games and add-ons for ​its console to be bought and sold only via its PlayStation Store, making prices higher than for physical games.  Sony says it has "invested years and billions" in an integrated gaming platform that benefits consumers in a competitive market, where rivals Nintendo and Microsoft's Xbox use similar models.

raw.headline: Sony fighting $2.7 billion UK lawsuit over PlayStation Store prices
raw.summary: Sony is fighting a London lawsuit worth almost 2 billion pounds ($2.7 billion) that alleges the PlayStation maker's "monopoly position" inflated prices ‌for digital games, in the latest mass consumer case to go to trial ‌in Britain.  The Japanese conglomerate is accused of abusing its dominant position by requiring digital games and add-ons for ​its console to be bought and sold only via its PlayStation Store, making prices higher than for physical games.  Sony says it has "invested years and billions" in an integrated gaming platform that benefits consumers in a competitive market, where rivals Nintendo and Microsoft's Xbox use similar models.

headline: Google to provide Pentagon with AI agents for unclassified work
summary: Google’s Gemini AI agents, which can undertake work independently on behalf of a user who sets them tasks, will initially operate on unclassified networks, said Emil Michael, the under secretary of defense for research and engineering.  “We’re starting with unclassified because that’s where most of the users are, and then we’ll get to classified and top secret,” Michael said in an interview, adding that talks with Google over using the agents on the classified cloud are underway.

headline: Inside Oracle's risky AI bet
summary: The race to build an AI ecosystem has propelled massive investments and brought the most unlikely players to the table. Oracle, the legacy enterprise software company launched in 1977 by co-founder Larry Ellison is one of them. It’s undergoing a massive transformation. Best known for its software database business, Oracle is now doubling down on AI cloud computing, but the jury is still out on whether Oracle will prove to be a winner. CNBC's Seema Mody has the story.

headline: TSMC Sales Grow 30% on Sustained Global Demand for AI Hardware
summary: The go-to chipmaker for Nvidia Corp., Advanced Micro Devices Inc. and Broadcom Inc. serves as a barometer for the global AI industry, with attention now on how the US and Israel’s strike on Iran will affect appetite to build data centers and other digital infrastructure.  The construction of AI data centers can cost tens of billions of dollars and require coordination with power generation and electrical grid operators, as well as suppliers and debt providers.  Oracle Corp. and OpenAI have scrapped plans to expand a flagship artificial intelligence data center in Texas after negotiations dragged over financing and OpenAI’s changing needs, Bloomberg News reported last week.

raw.headline: TSMC Sales Grow 30% on Sustained Global Demand for AI Hardware

The construction of AI data centers can cost tens of billions of dollars and require coordination with power generation and electrical grid operators, as well as suppliers and debt providers.  Oracle Corp. and OpenAI have scrapped plans to expand a flagship artificial intelligence data center in Texas after negotiations dragged over financing and OpenAI’s changing needs, Bloomberg News reported last week.

headline: Chai AI Announces Upcoming Rollout of Apple and Google Age Verification APIs to Enhance Platform Safety
summary: CHAI AI, the leading platform for conversational AI entertainment, today announced plans to implement native age verification systems leveraging Apple and Google's official APIs. This strategic integration, which will soon begin its phased rollout across applicable regions, reinforces CHAI AI's commitment to user safety and responsible AI engagement on both iOS and Android platforms.
